{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Manager",
  "description": "Smollan - We are a global commerce solutions company. We work at the pivot point where retailer, brand owner and shopper intercept. We help our clients achieve faster and scalable growth through planning, executing, measuring and reporting on all aspects of commerce.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager

Job Location: Nigeria

About the job


	Stakeholder Management
	Project Management
	Profitability&nbsp;
	Client/Customer Satisfaction
	Project Team Coordination.


Requirements


	Diploma or Degree / NQF level 7
	6-8 years of project management experience, FMCG exposure advantageous
	Essential / 5-10 years
	Communication skills (verbal &amp; written)
	Interpersonal skills
	Multi-tasking
	Administration skills
	Planning &amp; organising skills
	Project management skills
	Ability to work in a face-paced environment
	Time management
	Process oriented
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-15",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Project Manager Job at Smollan




        
    

        
        Smollan - We are a global commerce solutions company. We work at the pivot point where retailer, brand owner and shopper intercept. We help our clients achieve faster and scalable growth through planning, executing, measuring and reporting on all aspects of commerce.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager

Job Location: Nigeria

About the job


	Stakeholder Management
	Project Management
	Profitability 
	Client/Customer Satisfaction
	Project Team Coordination.


Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Diploma or Degree / NQF level 7
	6-8 years of project management experience, FMCG exposure advantageous
	Essential / 5-10 years
	Communication skills (verbal & written)
	Interpersonal skills
	Multi-tasking
	Administration skills
	Planning & organising skills
	Project management skills
	Ability to work in a face-paced environment
	Time management
	Process oriented
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Administrative Officer",
  "description": "CMP Nigeria Limited (CMP) is aconstruction project management company in Lagos. Our services include project management, construction management, and site development, all delivered with innovation, sustainability, and excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Dolphin Estate, Ikoyi - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are seeking a proactive Administrative Officer to oversee daily office operations, support management, and ensure smooth workflow across departments.
	The ideal candidate will be detail-oriented, resourceful, and capable of handling diverse administrative tasks with professionalism.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate and manage office activities to ensure efficiency
	Prepare, organize, and maintain records, reports, and documentation
	Support HR functions including recruitment, onboarding, and staff welfare
	Assist in budget preparation, expense tracking, and procurement processes
	Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders to facilitate communication
	Ensure compliance with company policies and procedures
	Provide general administrative support to senior management.


Requirements


	Minimum of an HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, or related fields
	2&ndash;3 years of proven experience in administrative or office management roles
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
	Proficiency in MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	Must reside close to Dolphin Estate, Ikoyi, Lagos.


Salary
N80,000 - N120,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: job@cmpnl.co.uk using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Administrative Officer Job at CMP Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        CMP Nigeria Limited (CMP) is aconstruction project management company in Lagos. Our services include project management, construction management, and site development, all delivered with innovation, sustainability, and excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Dolphin Estate, Ikoyi - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive Administrative Officer to oversee daily office operations, support management, and ensure smooth workflow across departments.
	The ideal candidate will be detail-oriented, resourceful, and capable of handling diverse administrative tasks with professionalism.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate and manage office activities to ensure efficiency
	Prepare, organize, and maintain records, reports, and documentation
	Support HR functions including recruitment, onboarding, and staff welfare
	Assist in budget preparation, expense tracking, and procurement processes
	Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders to facilitate communication
	Ensure compliance with company policies and procedures
	Provide general administrative support to senior management.


Requirements


	Minimum of an HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Management, or related fields
	2–3 years of proven experience in administrative or office management roles
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
	Proficiency in MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	Must reside close to Dolphin Estate, Ikoyi, Lagos.


Salary
N80,000 - N120,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Auditor",
  "description": "Fhemfel Group is a multifaceted company specializing in real estate, travel, and hospitality. We are dedicated to creating exceptional experiences and spaces that cater to the diverse needs of our clients. With a team of experts in each sector, we focus on delivering innovative solutions that combine luxury, comfort, and sustainability. Our commitment to quality and customer satisfaction drives us to excel in every project we undertake, making us a trusted partner in the industries we serve.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor
Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking a diligent Internal Auditor to join our real estate firm.
	The successful candidate will evaluate and enhance the effectiveness of internal controls, risk management, and governance processes across the company.
	This role ensures compliance with company policies, financial regulations, and operational standards within the real estate sector.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct periodic internal audits of financial, operational, and administrative processes within the real estate business.
	Review financial records, transactions, and reports for accuracy and compliance.
	Assess internal control systems and identify areas of risk or weakness.
	Ensure adherence to company policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare audit reports detailing findings, risks, and actionable recommendations.
	Follow up on audit findings to confirm corrective actions are implemented.
	Support management in fraud prevention and detection initiatives.
	Collaborate with departments to
	enhance processes and operational efficiency.
	Maintain comprehensive documentation of audit activities and evidence.
	Assist during external audits as required.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in internal audit or a related role.
	Professional certification (ICAN, ACCA, CIA) is an advantage.
	Strong understanding of internal controls, auditing standards, and risk management.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Excellent analytical and report-writing skills.
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel and accounting/audit software.


Key Competencies:


	Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
	Strong attention to detail
	Ethical judgment and integrity
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills


Method of Application&nbsp;

Internal Auditor Job at Fhemfel Group




        
    

        
        Fhemfel Group is a multifaceted company specializing in real estate, travel, and hospitality. We are dedicated to creating exceptional experiences and spaces that cater to the diverse needs of our clients. With a team of experts in each sector, we focus on delivering innovative solutions that combine luxury, comfort, and sustainability. Our commitment to quality and customer satisfaction drives us to excel in every project we undertake, making us a trusted partner in the industries we serve.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor
Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a diligent Internal Auditor to join our real estate firm.
	The successful candidate will evaluate and enhance the effectiveness of internal controls, risk management, and governance processes across the company.
	This role ensures compliance with company policies, financial regulations, and operational standards within the real estate sector.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct periodic internal audits of financial, operational, and administrative processes within the real estate business.
	Review financial records, transactions, and reports for accuracy and compliance.
	Assess internal control systems and identify areas of risk or weakness.
	Ensure adherence to company policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare audit reports detailing findings, risks, and actionable recommendations.
	Follow up on audit findings to confirm corrective actions are implemented.
	Support management in fraud prevention and detection initiatives.
	Collaborate with departments to
	enhance processes and operational efficiency.
	Maintain comprehensive documentation of audit activities and evidence.
	Assist during external audits as required.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in internal audit or a related role.
	Professional certification (ICAN, ACCA, CIA) is an advantage.
	Strong understanding of internal controls, auditing standards, and risk management.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Excellent analytical and report-writing skills.
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel and accounting/audit software.


Key Competencies:


	Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
	Strong attention to detail
	Ethical judgment and integrity
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills


Method of Application 
Interested an qualified candidates should send their application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Zylus Group International is a fast-growing real estate firm in Nigeria. We are a privately owned and internationally recognized business Capital organization with interests in Real Estate Investment, Property Sales, Human Capital Development, Agriculture, Infrastructure and General Construction, Property Valuation, Designs and Printing, Media, Transport and Logistics, Traveling and Tours, Property Management and many more.

The company&#39;s operation is governed and guided by a strict Code of Ethics which sets the pace for its operations, commitment to excellence, and professionalism. With the support of her committed team, Zylus Group International has grown tremendously in revenue, size, and strength. While maximizing the business potentials of her esteem valued Customer base, both locally and internationally.

The company aims to become one of Africa&#39;s most prominent conglomerates with a mandate to control over 80 independently-financed subsidiaries in five countries worldwide within its first ten years of operation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Awoyaya, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and ensure all transactions are properly documented.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly financial reports for management review.
	Monitor and reconcile bank accounts, cash transactions, and company ledgers.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable, ensuring timely payments and collections.
	Track production costs, including raw materials, labor, fuel, and other operational expenses.
	Monitor inventory of raw materials and finished products and reconcile stock records regularly.
	Record and reconcile daily sales transactions and customer payments.
	Prepare and process employee payroll and statutory deductions.
	Ensure timely remittance of taxes, pensions, and other statutory obligations.
	Assist in budget preparation and monitor adherence to approved budgets.
	Conduct periodic financial analysis and provide recommendations for cost reduction and profitability improvement.
	Support internal and external audits by providing required financial documentation.
	Maintain effective internal controls to safeguard company assets and prevent financial irregularities.
	Prepare management reports and provide financial insights to support decision-making.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, accounting standards, and regulatory requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	1&ndash;2 years of accounting experience, preferably in a manufacturing, construction, or block production environment.
	Professional certification such as ICAN, ACCA, or ATS is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles, financial reporting, and cost accounting.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software.
	Experience in inventory management and stock reconciliation.
	Excellent analytical, numerical, and problem-solving skills.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.


Salary
N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application to: hrrecruitment@zylusgroup.com using the Job Position &amp; Location as the subject of the mail.",

Accountant Job at Zylus Group




        
    

        
        Zylus Group International is a fast-growing real estate firm in Nigeria. We are a privately owned and internationally recognized business Capital organization with interests in Real Estate Investment, Property Sales, Human Capital Development, Agriculture, Infrastructure and General Construction, Property Valuation, Designs and Printing, Media, Transport and Logistics, Traveling and Tours, Property Management and many more.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



The company's operation is governed and guided by a strict Code of Ethics which sets the pace for its operations, commitment to excellence, and professionalism. With the support of her committed team, Zylus Group International has grown tremendously in revenue, size, and strength. While maximizing the business potentials of her esteem valued Customer base, both locally and internationally.

The company aims to become one of Africa's most prominent conglomerates with a mandate to control over 80 independently-financed subsidiaries in five countries worldwide within its first ten years of operation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Awoyaya, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and ensure all transactions are properly documented.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly financial reports for management review.
	Monitor and reconcile bank accounts, cash transactions, and company ledgers.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable, ensuring timely payments and collections.
	Track production costs, including raw materials, labor, fuel, and other operational expenses.
	Monitor inventory of raw materials and finished products and reconcile stock records regularly.
	Record and reconcile daily sales transactions and customer payments.
	Prepare and process employee payroll and statutory deductions.
	Ensure timely remittance of taxes, pensions, and other statutory obligations.
	Assist in budget preparation and monitor adherence to approved budgets.
	Conduct periodic financial analysis and provide recommendations for cost reduction and profitability improvement.
	Support internal and external audits by providing required financial documentation.
	Maintain effective internal controls to safeguard company assets and prevent financial irregularities.
	Prepare management reports and provide financial insights to support decision-making.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, accounting standards, and regulatory requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	1–2 years of accounting experience, preferably in a manufacturing, construction, or block production environment.
	Professional certification such as ICAN, ACCA, or ATS is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles, financial reporting, and cost accounting.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software.
	Experience in inventory management and stock reconciliation.
	Excellent analytical, numerical, and problem-solving skills.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.


Salary
N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application to: [email protected] using the Job Position & Location as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Jodazen Integrated Services
content:
Jodazen Integrated Services is a trusted partner for businesses and individuals seeking seamless HR and travel solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Human Resources Manager


Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Human Resources
Reports To: Managing Director / Chief Operating Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    





About the Role


	We are seeking a mature, emotionally intelligent HR professional to oversee and manage all aspects of the Human Resources function.
	The HR Manager will be responsible for building a positive workplace culture, ensuring HR policies and compliance, and supporting business growth through effective people management.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR policies, procedures, and systems aligned with company goals.
	Oversee full-cycle recruitment, onboarding, and employee retention strategies.
	Manage employee relations, conflict resolution, and disciplinary processes with emotional intelligence.
	Administer payroll, benefits, leave management, and statutory remittances in line with Nigerian Labour Law.
	Drive performance management, training, and career development programs.
	Ensure compliance with all labour laws, PENCOM, NSITF, ITF, and other regulatory requirements.
	Maintain confidential employee records and HR documentation.
	Provide HR reports and insights to MD/COO to support strategic decision-making.


Required Qualifications & Experience


	Education:
	
		Bachelor’s Degree in HR, Psychology, Business Administration, or related field.
		Minimum of 2nd Class Lower / Upper Credit HND.
	
	
	Experience: Minimum 2 years in a similar HR role with hands-on experience.
	Certification: CIPM certification is an added advantage.
	Age: 30 years and above.
	Gender: Male / Female – all qualified candidates encouraged to apply.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	High emotional intelligence and maturity in handling sensitive issues.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
	Leadership and team management skills.
	Execution excellence and strong attention to detail.
	Knowledge of Nigerian Labour Act and HR best practices.


Preferred Background:


	Real Estate industry experience is an advantage.







Remuneration


	Salary: N400,000 Net monthly - take-home after PAYE, employee pension, and NHF deductions.
	Other Benefits: HMO, Pension, Bonuses, and other statutory benefits.




Application Closing Date: 29th June, 2026.



Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Application – Human Resources Manager” as the subject of the email.


 

2.) Civil Engineer (Experienced)


Job Location: Lekki 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary


	We are seeking a competent and experienced Civil Engineer to join the team.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for planning, supervising, and coordinating construction projects to ensure quality delivery, compliance with regulatory standards, cost efficiency, and timely completion.
	The role requires hands-on experience in building construction and infrastructure development within the real estate sector.


Key Responsibilities
Project Planning and Execution:


	Supervise and coordinate construction activities on assigned projects.
	Review engineering drawings, specifications, and construction plans.
	Monitor project progress and ensure adherence to approved timelines.
	Conduct site inspections and provide technical guidance to site teams.


Site Supervision and Quality Control:


	Ensure all construction works are executed according to approved designs and quality standards.
	Monitor workmanship, material usage, and construction methodologies.
	Identify and resolve technical issues arising during project execution.
	Prepare site reports and maintain accurate project records.


Regulatory Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with Nigerian building regulations, safety standards, and environmental requirements.
	Liaise with consultants, contractors, and regulatory authorities as required.
	Assist in obtaining necessary approvals and permits for construction activities.


Cost and Resource Management:


	Monitor project costs and material utilization to minimize waste.
	Assist in project budgeting, cost estimation, and procurement planning.
	Review contractor work progress and recommend payments where applicable.


Health and Safety:


	Ensure strict adherence to health and safety policies on construction sites.
	Conduct regular safety inspections and recommend corrective actions.
	Promote a culture of safety among contractors and site personnel.


Stakeholder Coordination:


	Collaborate with architects, quantity surveyors, contractors, and other project stakeholders.
	Participate in project meetings and provide technical recommendations.
	Prepare and submit project progress reports to management.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree (B.Sc./B.Eng.) in Civil Engineering from a recognized institution.
	Membership of the Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE) is an added advantage.
	COREN registration or evidence of progression towards registration is an added advantage.


Experience:


	3–5 years of relevant experience in civil engineering, preferably within the real estate, construction, or property development industry.
	Proven experience in supervising residential, commercial, or mixed-use construction projects.
	Experience in site management, quality control, and project coordination.


Required Skills and Competencies
Technical Skills:


	Proficiency in AutoCAD and other engineering design software.
	Strong understanding of construction processes, structural works, and infrastructure development.
	Knowledge of Nigerian building codes and construction standards.
	Ability to interpret technical drawings and specifications.


Soft Skills:


	Strong leadership and supervisory skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Good problem-solving and analytical abilities.
	Strong organizational and project management skills.
	Ability to work independently and within a team environment.
	Attention to detail and commitment to quality.


Preferred Industry Experience:


	Candidates with experience in real estate development, residential estates, commercial buildings, infrastructure projects, and property construction will be highly preferred.




Salary
N500,000 - N600,000 monthly.



Application Closing Date: 29th June, 2026.



Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Civil Engineer" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.


 

3.)  Junior Civil Engineer



Job Location: Lekki 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary


	We are recruiting on behalf. We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Civil Engineer to support the planning, execution, and supervision of construction projects within our client's real estate development portfolio.
	The successful candidate will assist in ensuring projects are delivered according to specifications, quality standards, and timelines while gaining hands-on experience in building and infrastructure development.


Key Responsibilities
Site Supervision:


	Assist in supervising daily construction activities on project sites.
	Monitor site operations to ensure compliance with approved drawings and specifications.
	Conduct routine site inspections and report project progress to management.
	Support senior engineers in resolving technical and construction-related issues.


Quality Assurance:


	Monitor workmanship and material quality on-site.
	Ensure construction activities meet company quality standards.
	Assist in documenting site observations and quality control reports.


Project Coordination:


	Coordinate with contractors, suppliers, and consultants to ensure smooth project execution.
	Assist in tracking project schedules and reporting delays or challenges.
	Participate in project meetings and provide updates on assigned tasks.


Health, Safety, and Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with health and safety regulations on construction sites.
	Support the implementation of safety procedures and best practices.
	Assist in ensuring compliance with relevant building regulations and standards.


Documentation and Reporting:


	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly site reports.
	Maintain project records, drawings, and technical documentation.
	Assist in preparing project progress reports for management review.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree (B.Sc./B.Eng.) in Civil Engineering from a recognized institution.
	NYSC completion certificate is required.
	Membership of the Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE) is an added advantage.


Experience:


	1–2 years of relevant experience in civil engineering, construction, or real estate development.
	Experience in site supervision and project coordination is an advantage.
	Fresh graduates with strong internship or industrial training experience may also be considered.


Required Skills and Competencies:
Technical Skills:


	Basic proficiency in AutoCAD and Microsoft Office applications.
	Understanding of construction processes and engineering principles.
	Ability to read and interpret engineering drawings.
	Knowledge of site supervision and quality control practices.


Soft Skills:


	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong attention to detail.
	Ability to work effectively in a team environment.
	Good problem-solving and analytical skills.
	Willingness to learn and take initiative.


Preferred Industry Experience:


	Candidates with experience or exposure to real estate development, residential construction projects, estate infrastructure, and building construction will be an added advantage.




Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.



Application Closing Date: 30th June, 2026.



Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Junior Civil Engineer" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
